revision.comment: Text replacement - "8年" to "8th year "
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}})Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:度会国御神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]][[ja:度会国御神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}})Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:度会国御神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]][[ja:度会国御神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

{{Shrine | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}})Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]][[ja:度会国御神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:度会国御神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]][[ja:度会国御神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}})Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:度会国御神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]][[ja:度会国御神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=draft}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=draft}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|12=draft}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|12=draft}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=draft}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=draft}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}})Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|12=draft}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|12=draft}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|12=draft}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|12=draft}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|12=draft}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|12=draft}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]]

Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|12=draft}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|12=draft}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|12=draft}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=draft}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=draft}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|12=draft}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|12=draft}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=draft}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=draft}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}})Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|12=draft}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|12=draft}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|12=draft}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|12=draft}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|12=draft}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|12=draft}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=draft}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=draft}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|12=draft}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|12=draft}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=draft}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=draft}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}})Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|12=draft}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|12=draft}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|12=draft}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|12=draft}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|12=draft}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|12=draft}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]]

Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|12=draft}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|12=draft}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|12=draft}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=draft|12=draft}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|12=draft|12=draft}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|12=draft|12=draft}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=draft|12=draft}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}})Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft|12=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=draft|12=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|12=draft|12=draft}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|12=draft|12=draft}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|12=draft|12=draft}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|12=draft|12=draft}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|12=draft|12=draft}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=draft|12=draft}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|12=draft|12=draft}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|12=draft|12=draft}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=draft|12=draft}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}})Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft|12=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=draft|12=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|12=draft|12=draft}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|12=draft|12=draft}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|12=draft|12=draft}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|12=draft|12=draft}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|12=draft|12=draft}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]]

He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=draft|12=draft}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}})Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft|12=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=draft|12=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon.
