Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft|12=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=draft|12=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|12=draft|12=draft}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|12=draft|12=draft}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|12=draft|12=draft}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|12=draft|12=draft}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|12=draft|12=draft}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] to [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=draft|12=draft}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|12=draft|12=draft}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|12=draft|12=draft}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=draft|12=draft}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}})Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft|12=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=draft|12=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|12=draft|12=draft}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|12=draft|12=draft}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|12=draft|12=draft}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|12=draft|12=draft}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|12=draft|12=draft}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]]

The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|12=draft|12=draft}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|12=draft|12=draft}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|12=draft|12=draft}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|12=draft" to "|qq=draft"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}})Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]]

revision.comment: 22 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:度会国御神社 up to rev 90836368
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 度会国御神社 |画像= [[File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]] |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.8 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 7.6 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 彦國見賀岐建與束命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
[[豊受大神宮]]摂社 |創建= [[雄略天皇]]22年以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 度会国都御神の社 |札所等= |例祭= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月19日]]） |神事= }} '''度会国御神社'''（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[豊受大神宮]]（外宮）の摂社。外宮の宮域内にあり、北御門脇の小道沿いに鎮座する[[アイティービー|iTV]]"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php 度会国御神社|伊勢神宮百科辞典|伊勢志摩の旅よいとこせ]"（2011年4月3日閲覧。）。 == 概要 == [[伊勢市駅]]より南西へ約900[[メートル|m]]式内社研究会 編（1990）：320ページ、[[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]]の伊勢神宮外宮境内に鎮座する。外宮の摂社としては[[草奈伎神社|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社]]に次ぐ3位である。 [[祭神]]は彦國見賀岐建與束命宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ（ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみこと伊勢文化舎（2008）：37ページ）。鎮座地である伊勢国の[[国魂神]]で、代々伊勢北部を支配してきた[[伊勢国造]]の始祖、[[天日別命]]（あめのひわけのみこと）の子とされる。母については知られない。 [[社殿]]は[[神明造]]の[[板葺]]で、写真にあるように[[玉垣]]に囲まれている式内社研究会 編（1990）：322ページ。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。度会国御神社の更に先には、外宮末社の[[大津神社 (伊勢市)|大津神社]]が鎮座する。 == 歴史 == 度会国都御神の社（わたらいのくにつみかみのやしろ）と称していたが、現在は『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載されている名称に従い、度会国御神社と表記される櫻井（1998）：86ページ。 創建年代は不詳である式内社研究会 編（1990）：321ページが、外宮の鎮座（[[雄略天皇]]22年）以前よりあったと考えられている。[[櫻井勝之進]]は度会国御神社に関連して、以下のように記述している。 {{Quotation|一つの社会が[[ヤマト王権|中央の政権]]のもとに統合されその国家の組織の中に組み込まれるならば、そこで祭っていた神々もまた国家至上の大神と何らかの神系あるいは神縁の神々として組織化されることは、たとえば[[古代出雲|出雲]]と中央の関係を描いた[[神話]]にも示されている。 |櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』35ページ}} すなわち、[[磯部 (部民)|磯部]]の有力者であった度会氏に[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大御神]]の祭祀をゆだねた櫻井（1998）：35ページ際に、度会氏が祭っていた神々も伊勢神宮の中に組み込まれたものと考えられる。 創建当初の鎮座地は[[山田 (伊勢市)|山田]]前田村であったとされ、『二宮管社沿革考』は、前野村住民は[[産土神]]として度会国御神社を奉仕したと記している。[[中世]]には[[世義寺]]が境内に建てられたことにより一時祭祀が断絶するが、[[江戸時代]]の[[寛文]]年中（[[1661年]] - [[1672年]]）に世義寺が移転し、度会国御神社の神霊も同地に移された。移転前の世義寺は、現在の伊勢市八日市場町、神宮山田工作所付近にあった。後に度会国御神社の遥拝所であった現在地に、寺から分離して遷座したという。 社殿は[[1956年]]（昭和31年）12月に建て替えが行われ、[[1980年]]（昭和55年）[[4月10日]]に大規模な修繕がなされた。 社地からは[[陶器]]の残欠が見つかっている伊勢市 編（2011）：364ページ。見つかった陶器は、ほとんどが[[須恵器]]である伊勢市 編（2011）：370ページ。 == 祭祀 == [[6月19日]]と[[12月19日]]の[[月次祭]][[神饌|大御饌]]・[[奉幣]]、[[10月19日]]の[[神嘗祭]]大御饌・奉幣、[[11月25日]]の[[新嘗祭]]大御饌・奉幣は度会国御神社で祭祀を執行し、[[歳旦祭]]（[[1月1日]]）、[[元始祭]]（[[1月3日]]）、建国記念祭（[[2月11日]]）、[[祈年祭]]（[[2月19日]]）、風日祈祭（[[5月14日]]・[[8月4日]]）、[[天長祭]]（[[12月23日]]）は外宮五丈殿での遥祀を行う。 == 交通 == ;外宮前まで * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]]伊勢市駅南口（JR）より徒歩5分程度。 * [[三重交通]]・[[三交伊勢志摩交通]]・[[伊勢市コミュニティバス|おかげバス]]「伊勢市駅前」バス停より徒歩5分。 * 三重交通「外宮前」バス停よりすぐ。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北へ約5分。北御門前に434台収容の[[無料]][[駐車場]]あり三重県観光連盟"[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html 三重バリアフリーレジャーガイド【伊勢志摩】おかげ横丁]"（2011年4月11日閲覧。）。 ;外宮宮域内 * 御厩前の小路を西進すると神社が現れる伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：36ページ。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23年3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国造]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:わたらいくにみしんしや}} [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 度会国御神社 |画像= [[File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]] |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.8 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 7.6 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 彦國見賀岐建與束命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
[[豊受大神宮]]摂社 |創建= [[雄略天皇]]22年以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 度会国都御神の社 |札所等= |例祭= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月19日]]） |神事= }} '''度会国御神社'''（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[豊受大神宮]]（外宮）の摂社。外宮の宮域内にあり、北御門脇の小道沿いに鎮座する[[アイティービー|iTV]]"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php 度会国御神社|伊勢神宮百科辞典|伊勢志摩の旅よいとこせ]"（2011年4月3日閲覧。）。 == 概要 == [[伊勢市駅]]より南西へ約900[[メートル|m]]式内社研究会 編（1990）：320ページ、[[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]]の伊勢神宮外宮境内に鎮座する。外宮の摂社としては[[草奈伎神社|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社]]に次ぐ3位である。 [[祭神]]は彦國見賀岐建與束命宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ（ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみこと伊勢文化舎（2008）：37ページ）。鎮座地である伊勢国の[[国魂神]]で、代々伊勢北部を支配してきた[[伊勢国造]]の始祖、[[天日別命]]（あめのひわけのみこと）の子とされる。母については知られない。 [[社殿]]は[[神明造]]の[[板葺]]で、写真にあるように[[玉垣]]に囲まれている式内社研究会 編（1990）：322ページ。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。度会国御神社の更に先には、外宮末社の[[大津神社 (伊勢市)|大津神社]]が鎮座する。 == 歴史 == 度会国都御神の社（わたらいのくにつみかみのやしろ）と称していたが、現在は『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載されている名称に従い、度会国御神社と表記される櫻井（1998）：86ページ。 創建年代は不詳である式内社研究会 編（1990）：321ページが、外宮の鎮座（[[雄略天皇]]22年）以前よりあったと考えられている。[[櫻井勝之進]]は度会国御神社に関連して、以下のように記述している。 {{Quotation|一つの社会が[[ヤマト王権|中央の政権]]のもとに統合されその国家の組織の中に組み込まれるならば、そこで祭っていた神々もまた国家至上の大神と何らかの神系あるいは神縁の神々として組織化されることは、たとえば[[古代出雲|出雲]]と中央の関係を描いた[[神話]]にも示されている。 |櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』35ページ}} すなわち、[[磯部 (部民)|磯部]]の有力者であった度会氏に[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大御神]]の祭祀をゆだねた櫻井（1998）：35ページ際に、度会氏が祭っていた神々も伊勢神宮の中に組み込まれたものと考えられる。 創建当初の鎮座地は[[山田 (伊勢市)|山田]]前田村であったとされ、『二宮管社沿革考』は、前野村住民は[[産土神]]として度会国御神社を奉仕したと記している。[[中世]]には[[世義寺]]が境内に建てられたことにより一時祭祀が断絶するが、[[江戸時代]]の[[寛文]]年中（[[1661年]] - [[1672年]]）に世義寺が移転し、度会国御神社の神霊も同地に移された。移転前の世義寺は、現在の伊勢市八日市場町、神宮山田工作所付近にあった。後に度会国御神社の遥拝所であった現在地に、寺から分離して遷座したという。 社殿は[[1956年]]（昭和31年）12月に建て替えが行われ、[[1980年]]（昭和55年）[[4月10日]]に大規模な修繕がなされた。 社地からは[[陶器]]の残欠が見つかっている伊勢市 編（2011）：364ページ。見つかった陶器は、ほとんどが[[須恵器]]である伊勢市 編（2011）：370ページ。 == 祭祀 == [[6月19日]]と[[12月19日]]の[[月次祭]][[神饌|大御饌]]・[[奉幣]]、[[10月19日]]の[[神嘗祭]]大御饌・奉幣、[[11月25日]]の[[新嘗祭]]大御饌・奉幣は度会国御神社で祭祀を執行し、[[歳旦祭]]（[[1月1日]]）、[[元始祭]]（[[1月3日]]）、建国記念祭（[[2月11日]]）、[[祈年祭]]（[[2月19日]]）、風日祈祭（[[5月14日]]・[[8月4日]]）、[[天長祭]]（[[12月23日]]）は外宮五丈殿での遥祀を行う。 == 交通 == ;外宮前まで * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]]伊勢市駅南口（JR）より徒歩5分程度。 * [[三重交通]]・[[三交伊勢志摩交通]]・[[伊勢市コミュニティバス|おかげバス]]「伊勢市駅前」バス停より徒歩5分。 * 三重交通「外宮前」バス停よりすぐ。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北へ約5分。北御門前に434台収容の[[無料]][[駐車場]]あり三重県観光連盟"[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html 三重バリアフリーレジャーガイド【伊勢志摩】おかげ横丁]"（2011年4月11日閲覧。）。 ;外宮宮域内 * 御厩前の小路を西進すると神社が現れる伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：36ページ。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23年3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国造]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:わたらいくにみしんしや}} [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}})Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|度会国御神社|version=90836368|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会国御神社|version=90836368|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}})Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会国御神社|version=90836368|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]]

Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会国御神社|version=90836368|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}})Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会国御神社|version=90836368|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]]

Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okage Bus|qq=}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Dokaikoku Shrine]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Station|qq=}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Kokumika Shrine|qq=}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|qq=}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|qq=}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|qq=}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki|qq=}})Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsunosuke|qq=}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe|qq=}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada, Ise|qq=}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}}. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Segi-ji Temple|qq=}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|qq=}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmikesai|qq=}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōhei|qq=}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|qq=}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangū Line|qq=}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okage Bus|qq=}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会国御神社|version=90836368|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[sn:Watarai Kunimi Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Kunimi Shrine]]

Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmikesai|qq=}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōhei|qq=}}) on June 19 and December 19; the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} on October 19; and the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|qq=}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangū Line|qq=}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okage Bus|qq=}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}, heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p.
