[[ja:度会国御神社]] [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[ja:度会国御神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11486118]] Wikidata name: Watara Kunimi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|WD=Q17223204}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.488833|N|136.702111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[:en:ITV|ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]]Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Kokumika Shrine|1=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486118}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633343}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaki Shrine|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|1=Amihiwake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106107046}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]])Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsunosuke|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 磯部|1=Isobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588058}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada, Ise|1=Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470062}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Segi-ji Temple|1=Segi-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362520}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|1=Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391248}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmikesai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōhei|qq=}}) on June 19 and December 19; the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] on October 19; and the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okage Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢市コミュニティバス|1=Okage Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379021}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会国御神社|version=90836368|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine (Q11486118)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|WD=Q11486118}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.488814|136.702152|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181143 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11486118}}==Notes== [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:度会国御神社]] [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[ja:度会国御神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11486118]] Wikidata name: Watara Kunimi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|WD=Q17223204}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.488833|N|136.702111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[:en:ITV|ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]]Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Kokumika Shrine|1=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486118}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633343}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaki Shrine|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|1=Amihiwake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106107046}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]])Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsunosuke|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 磯部|1=Isobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588058}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada, Ise|1=Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470062}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Segi-ji Temple|1=Segi-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362520}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|1=Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391248}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmikesai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōhei|qq=}}) on June 19 and December 19; the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] on October 19; and the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okage Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢市コミュニティバス|1=Okage Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379021}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会国御神社|version=90836368|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:度会国御神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine (Q11486118)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|WD=Q11486118}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.488814|136.702152|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181143 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11486118}}==Notes== [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:度会国御神社]] [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[ja:度会国御神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11486118]] Wikidata name: Watara Kunimi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|WD=Q17223204}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.488833|N|136.702111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[:en:ITV|ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]]Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Kokumika Shrine|1=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486118}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633343}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaki Shrine|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|1=Amihiwake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106107046}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]])Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsunosuke|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 磯部|1=Isobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588058}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada, Ise|1=Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470062}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Segi-ji Temple|1=Segi-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362520}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|1=Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391248}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmikesai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōhei|qq=}}) on June 19 and December 19; the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] on October 19; and the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okage Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢市コミュニティバス|1=Okage Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379021}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会国御神社|version=90836368|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:度会国御神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine (Q11486118)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|WD=Q11486118}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.488814|136.702152|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181143 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11486118}}==Notes== [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:度会国御神社]] [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[ja:度会国御神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11486118]] Wikidata name: Watara Kunimi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|WD=Q17223204}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.488833|N|136.702111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[:en:ITV|ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]]Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Kokumika Shrine|1=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486118}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633343}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaki Shrine|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|1=Amihiwake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106107046}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]])Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsunosuke|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 磯部|1=Isobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588058}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada, Ise|1=Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470062}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Segi-ji Temple|1=Segi-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362520}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|1=Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391248}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmikesai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōhei|qq=}}) on June 19 and December 19; the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] on October 19; and the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okage Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会国御神社|version=90836368|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:度会国御神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine (Q11486118)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|WD=Q11486118}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.488814|136.702152|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181143 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11486118}}==Notes== [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:度会国御神社]] [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[ja:度会国御神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11486118]] Wikidata name: Watara Kunimi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|WD=Q17223204}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.488833|N|136.702111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[:en:ITV|ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]]Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Kokumika Shrine|1=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486118}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633343}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaki Shrine|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|1=Amihiwake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106107046}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]])Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsunosuke|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 磯部|1=Isobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588058}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada, Ise|1=Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470062}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Segi-ji Temple|1=Segi-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362520}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|1=Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391248}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmikesai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōhei|qq=}}) on June 19 and December 19; the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] on October 19; and the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okage Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢市コミュニティバス|1=Okage Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379021}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会国御神社|version=90836368|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:度会国御神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine (Q11486118)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|WD=Q11486118}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.488814|136.702152|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181143 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11486118}}==Notes== [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11486118]] Wikidata name: Watara Kunimi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Watarai Kunimi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Watarai Kunimi Shrine.jpg | location = Toyokawa-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 19.8 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 42 | Longitude_Sec = 7.6 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Hikokunimikaki Takeroyorozu-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine)
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Shrine of Watarai Kunitsu-no-Mikami | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 19) | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|WD=Q17223204}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.488833|N|136.702111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine''' (度会国御神社, *Watarai Kunimi Jinja*) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, *Gekū*). It is located within the precincts of the Gekū, along a small path beside the North Gate.[[:en:ITV|ITV]] "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Watarai Kunimi Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed April 3, 2011) == Overview == Located about 900 meters southwest of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]]Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 320, {{ill|Watarai Kokumika Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Kokumika Shrine|1=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486118}} sits within the precincts of the {{ill|Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633343}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks third after {{ill|Kusanaki Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaki Shrine|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} and {{ill|Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmakuni Musubi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦國見賀岐建與束命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 12 (ひこくにみがきたけよつかのみことIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 37), regarded as the {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂}} (guardian deity of the land) of Ise Province. He is said to be the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|1=Amihiwake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106107046}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, who historically governed northern Ise. His mother is unknown. The shrine building follows the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style, with a wooden plank roof, enclosed by a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]])Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 322. There is no offering box. Further beyond Watarai Kokumika Shrine lies the {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Gekū. == History == Originally known as the Shrine of Watarai no Kunitsumikami (度会国都御神の社), the name has been standardized as Watarai Kokumika Shrine following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}Sakurai (1998): p. 86. The exact date of its founding is unknownShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 321, but it is believed to predate the establishment of the Gekū during the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku’s reign. Regarding the shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsunosuke|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation's structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as gods related to the supreme deity of the nation, either through divine lineage or divine association, as depicted in myths regarding the relationship between ancient Izumo and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 磯部|1=Isobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588058}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}Sakurai (1998): p. 35, the deities they had been venerating were integrated into Ise Jingū’s pantheon. The shrine was initially located in {{ill|Yamada, Ise|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada, Ise|1=Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470062}} Maeda Village. According to *Ninomiya Kansha Enkaku Kō*, the villagers of Maeno served Watarai Kokumika Shrine as their [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. In the medieval period, the establishment of {{ill|Segi-ji Temple|ja|世義寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Segi-ji Temple|1=Segi-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362520}} within the precincts temporarily disrupted the shrine’s rituals. However, during the Kanbun era (1661–1672), Segi-ji was relocated, and the shrine's deity was also moved to the same site. Before its relocation, Segi-ji Temple was situated near present-day {{ill|Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|ja|八日市場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|1=Yōkaichiba-chō, Ise|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391248}}, close to the Jingū Yamada Workshop. Eventually, the shrine was separated from the temple and moved to its current location, which had served as its place of worship. The shrine building was reconstructed in December 1956 (Shōwa 31) and underwent major renovations on April 10, 1980 (Shōwa 55). Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery have been found at the shrine siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most of the discovered ceramics identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Rituals == Major festivals include the {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} with grand offerings ({{ill|Ōmikesai|ja|大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmikesai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and presentations ({{ill|Hōhei|ja|奉幣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōhei|qq=}}) on June 19 and December 19; the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] on October 19; and the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] on November 25. Other rituals, such as: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February 19) * {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May 14 & August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (December 23) These are conducted via remote worship at the Gojō-den Hall of the Gekū. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * 5-minute walk from JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station (south exit). * 5-minute walk from Mie Kōtsū, Sankō Ise-Shima Kōtsū, or {{ill|Okage Bus|ja|おかげバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okage Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢市コミュニティバス|1=Okage Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379021}} "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Immediate access from Mie Kōtsū's "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], heading north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road), with a free parking lot for 434 vehicles near the North GateMie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/sagasu12_barrier/barifuri/leisure/area_ise/01-jingu.html Mie Barrier-Free Leisure Guide]" (Accessed April 11, 2011). ;Within the Gekū Precinct * Proceed west along the path in front of the Gokura to reach the shrineIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 36. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国造]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0204.html 度会国御神社（わたらいくにみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会国御神社|version=90836368|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Watarai Kunimi Shrine (Q11486118)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|WD=Q11486118}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.488814|136.702152|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181143 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11486118}}==Notes== [[ja:度会国御神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
